---
title: "Astronomy from the Bottom of the World"
occasion: "Astronomy on Tap"
description: "SPT and CMB cosmology for a general audience"
author:
  name: "Cail Daley & Lennart Balkenhol"
  affiliation: "CosmoStat, CEA Paris-Saclay"
date: 2026-04-15
date-format: long

format:
  revealjs:
    width: 1920
    height: 1080
    theme: [default, custom.scss]
    slide-number: true
    fig-align: center
    menu: false
    include-after-body: assets/no_footer_on_titleslide.html
    footer: "Daley & Balkenhol | Astronomy on Tap | April 2026"
    history: false
    hash: true
    pdf-separate-fragments: true
    pdf-max-pages-per-slide: 1
    template-partials:
      - title-slide.html
    center-title-slide: false
# decktape: decktape reveal --fragments -s 1920x1080 _site/26_AoT_BottomOfTheWorld/index.html 26_AoT_BottomOfTheWorld/index.pdf

html-math-method:
  method: mathjax
  url: https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/mathjax@3/es5/tex-svg-full.js
include-in-header: assets/mathjax.html
---

<!-- ============================================================ -->
<!-- PART 1 — LENNART: The Universe & the CMB                     -->
<!-- ============================================================ -->

## The Big Bang left us a message {.full}

<!-- Planck CMB temperature map — stunning full-bleed image -->
<!-- images/Planck_2018_T_CMB.png -->

## The Universe has a recipe {.full}

<!-- Spergel components pie chart or similar -->
<!-- images/Spergel_components.png -->

## We can read it in the light {.full}

<!-- CMB power spectrum — FIRAS or TT spectrum -->
<!-- images/FIRAS.png -->

<!-- ============================================================ -->
<!-- PART 2 — CAIL: SPT — the instrument and the site            -->
<!-- ============================================================ -->

## Why go to the bottom of the world? {.full}

<!-- SPT site photo — South Pole, polar plateau, atmosphere -->

## The South Pole Telescope {.full}

<!-- Wide-angle SPT photo from bbenson gallery -->

## Inside the receiver {.full}

<!-- Focal plane / detector array photo -->

## The sky as SPT sees it {.full}

<!-- SPT temperature map or survey footprint -->

## What we're learning {.full}

<!-- Science result — lensing map or cluster catalog -->
